g.text: The real-data evaluation covers three scene families, with mixed scenes introducing the strongest cross-geometry ambiguity.

text.style: font-weight: 700; font-size: 11px; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'Times', 'DejaVu Serif', serif; text-anchor: start; fill: #1f3a5f

font-size: 9px; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'Times', 'DejaVu Serif', serif; text-anchor: start; fill: #333333

g.text: Estimate multiple structures, reject outliers, and preserve agreement with scene-level ground truth under one unified protocol.

text.style: font-weight: 700; font-size: 10.3px; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'Times', 'DejaVu Serif', serif; text-anchor: start; fill: #333333
font-size: 9.1px; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'Times', 'DejaVu Serif', serif; text-anchor: start; fill: #333333

g.text: ARI / NMI / label purity / IoU matching / runtime / privacy trade-off

text.style: font-size: 8.9px; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'Times', 'DejaVu Serif', serif; text-anchor: start; fill: #333333

g.text: Common output view: predicted labels, model count, fitting quality, and privacy cost

text.style: font-size: 9.2px; font-family: 'Times New Roman', 'Times', 'DejaVu Serif', serif; text-anchor: middle; fill: #333333

g.text: Mixed scenes are the most demanding because the evaluation must compare structures across different geometric families without changing the protocol.
